title: ZeroAvia to Build Manufacturing Hub in Scotland

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: First Practical Zero Emmision Aviation Powertrain | USA & UK | ZeroAvia ZeroAvia to Build Manufacturing Hub in Scotland | ZeroAvia News Scottish Enterprise have awarded ZeroAvia a £9m Regional Selective Assistance grant to help realise the project alongside company investment, creating hundreds of jobs The manufacturing plans build on UK Government R&D funding and equity investment into ZeroAvia Scotland’s First Minister John Swinney and UK Government Secretary of State for Scotland Ian Murray launches the new project alongside ZeroAvia CEO Val Miftakhov

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: [Everett, WA and Kemble, UK: May 21, 2025] — ZeroAvia today announced that it will build a major manufacturing facility for production of its hydrogen-electric powertrains at the Advanced Manufacturing Innovation District Scotland, close to Glasgow Airport in Renfrewshire. ZeroAvia’s Hydrogen Centre of Excellence will support the company in a range of manufacturing operations relating to powertrain production and testing and will also be the company’s primary location for production of advanced high temperature PEM (HTPEM) fuel cell stacks and supporting systems. With nearly 3,000 full engine and component pre-orders agreed with airlines and OEMs – corresponding to over $10 billion in future revenues – ZeroAvia is advancing its production operations to meet demand.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The new facility will complement ZeroAvia’s Propulsion Center of Excellence in Washington State which is focusing primarily on electric motors and power electronics. The company also has plans to expand its R&D and aircraft testing centre at Cotswold Airport in Gloucestershire.
